other_observers: Mark Peterson; I found and identified the bird
specific_location: south side of US 50 about 2 mi. west of town
description: An obvious Plegadis ibis, the bird was with 47 White-faced Ibis. The bird had obvious blue borders above and below the dark blue facial skin; the borders did not extend behind the eye. The bill was grayer than were the bills of the neighboring White-faced Ibis and the legs were mostly dark with just a suggestion of reddish ankles. The tertials were iridescent purplish-green and were concolorous with the greater coverts, unlike the tertials of the neighboring White-faced Ibis.Behaviors: Foraged in a flooded alfalfa field, probing into the ground.Call: none heardPlumage: Definitive Basic
